jokes.text: Well, apparently stabbing Iceman in the exercise yard couldn't wait till Monday.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explains her stepdad can't get weekend furlough for her wedding
jokes.punchline: The casual delivery of violent prison incident as reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - dark subject matter delivered with her characteristic obliviousness. The specific name 'Iceman' and Monday deadline add absurd specificity that elevates basic prison violence joke.

jokes.text: I am pretty 'wisdomous.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe says Joey has shared his wisdom with her
jokes.punchline: Joey's made-up word 'wisdomous' to describe himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey malapropism that feels authentic to his character. Not groundbreaking but well-executed character comedy.

jokes.text: I don't want you to see your father cry. Go to your room!
jokes.setup: Joey is emotional about walking Phoebe down the aisle
jokes.punchline: He tells Phoebe to go to her room so she won't see him cry
jokes.explanation: Sweet reversal of parent-child dynamic with Joey trying to be fatherly but getting overwhelmed. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Well, I don't care. So you pick. Did you just hang up on me?
jokes.setup: Monica as wedding planner is being controlling about fish selection
jokes.punchline: Monica's shock that someone would hang up on her during her controlling moment
jokes.explanation: Solid Monica control-freak moment but fairly predictable setup and payoff.

jokes.text: You don't know military time? I must have been in missile training the day they taught that.
jokes.setup: Monica uses military time and Mike doesn't understand
jokes.punchline: Mike's sarcastic excuse about being in missile training
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback that plays into Monica's over-organization without being too obvious.

jokes.text: Okay, so 1800 minus 12... is 1788.
jokes.setup: Monica tells Mike to subtract 12 from 1800 to get 6 PM
jokes.punchline: Mike gets 1788 instead of 6, showing terrible math
jokes.explanation: Simple math fail joke. Gets the job done but nothing special.

jokes.text: She will shower when Tibet is free.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions Marjorie's overwhelming scent
jokes.punchline: Comparison to Tibetan independence as timeline for bathing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, unexpected comparison that's both topical and absurd. Great economy and specificity elevate a basic hygiene joke.

jokes.text: I rarely practice my meals before I eat.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions they're glad to have a rehearsal dinner
jokes.punchline: Chandler's literal interpretation of 'rehearsal dinner'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler observational humor with good wordplay. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: That why your hand's against my crotch? - That is why.
jokes.setup: Chandler was going for a handshake but they're hugging
jokes.punchline: Awkward hand placement and Chandler's matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment that works but relies on basic awkwardness rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Are you still crying about your damn baby?
jokes.setup: Context suggests someone is emotional
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt, inappropriate comment about someone being sad about their baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - inappropriately blunt in a way that's shocking but true to character.

jokes.text: You see these clowns all the time.
jokes.setup: Monica is enforcing a 20-second per person greeting rule
jokes.punchline: Calling the wedding guests 'clowns' while being efficiency-focused
jokes.explanation: Good Monica control moment, though 'clowns' feels a bit forced as an insult.

jokes.text: Who in God's name are you? I'm not that fond of you either, buddy.
jokes.setup: Joey tries to bond with Mike's parents about kids growing up
jokes.punchline: Dad's hostile response and Joey's defensive comeback
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment with escalation but fairly standard meet-the-parents tension.

jokes.text: This is like figure skating team all over again. I mean, synchronized swimming. I mean... I mean, the balance beam.
jokes.setup: Chandler compares not making the wedding party to sports disappointment
jokes.punchline: His correction through increasingly feminine/individual sports
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke that reveals Chandler's insecurity while getting progressively more absurd with each 'correction.'

jokes.text: Help me. - Football.
jokes.setup: Chandler is stuck in his spiral of feminine sports
jokes.punchline: Ross saves him with 'football'
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback resolution with great economy. Ross's simple save makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I don't have a fax machine. Well, then there are gonna be a few surprises.
jokes.setup: Monica says she sent a fax about changing the vegetarian items
jokes.punchline: Monica's ominous response to Phoebe not having a fax
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery that sets up potential chaos. Monica's assumption about fax machines is very her.

jokes.text: We're sitting here, alone, doing nothing. It's our rehearsal for tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler feel left out of wedding preparations
jokes.punchline: Calling their exclusion a 'rehearsal' for being excluded tomorrow
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about rehearsal that works on multiple levels. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: Oh, I will. But I will need a mirror... as he is me.
jokes.setup: Chandler claims he'll say hello to Mike's next groomsman
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate way of saying it's him
jokes.explanation: Tries too hard to be clever. The structure is convoluted for a modest payoff.

jokes.text: You are going 'downer.'
jokes.setup: Ross says 'You're going down'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's attempt to one-up with 'downer'
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that doesn't really work. Feels forced and not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I wouldn't know. I didn't make it.
jokes.setup: Ross asks about figure skating team sayings
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan reminder that he didn't make the team
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with excellent economy and deadpan delivery. Self-deprecating humor works well.

jokes.text: Oh, a wiseacre. Strike two.
jokes.setup: Joey asks Mike's intentions, Mike says he intends to marry Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Joey treating it like baseball with strikes
jokes.explanation: Good setup for running gag. Joey's protective father act works with his character.

jokes.text: Hey, unless we move in with you, Dad.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Phoebe supporting Mike financially
jokes.punchline: Mike suggesting they could move in with Joey
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of the financial instability joke with a good callback to Joey being 'dad.'

jokes.text: You only get one more, Mike.
jokes.setup: Joey said 'Strike three' but Mike gets another chance
jokes.punchline: Joey's inconsistent baseball metaphor
jokes.explanation: Plays into Joey's confusion about his own metaphors. Mildly amusing but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Just not enough to put us in the original wedding party.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says she loves Ross and Chandler so much
jokes.punchline: Ross's bitter but accurate observation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that cuts through the sentiment with painful truth.

jokes.text: Is that what you say to Chandler?
jokes.setup: Monica says 'get in, do your thing, and get out' about speeches
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about Monica and Chandler's relationship
jokes.explanation: Basic sexual innuendo that relies on obvious setup. Fairly cheap humor.

jokes.text: It's not important. Well, she's in rehab.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says Mary Ellen couldn't be there and it's not important why
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals it's because she's in rehab
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - tries to be tactful then immediately overshares. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: You're fired! Cheers.
jokes.setup: Phoebe has emotional breakdown about wanting simple wedding
jokes.punchline: Firing Monica then immediately saying 'Cheers' as if toasting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of dramatic firing with casual 'Cheers.' The timing and economy make this excellent.

jokes.text: Man! I wouldn't have had breakfast if I knew there was gonna be corsages.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions moving corsages to make room in fridge
jokes.punchline: Joey's implication that he would eat corsages
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey food obsession joke. The logic is absurd but completely consistent with his character. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I've won awards... I printed them out on my computer.
jokes.setup: Monica brags about winning awards for organizational skills
jokes.punchline: Reveals she made the awards herself
jokes.explanation: Great deflation moment. Monica's pride followed by admission of self-awarded honors is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: My God, you're breathtaking.
jokes.setup: Ross comes to talk to Rachel about groomsman situation
jokes.punchline: He's distracted by how she looks and forgets his purpose
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection that plays into Ross's history with Rachel. Serviceable but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Oh, you are the lesser of two evils.
jokes.setup: Ross argues why Rachel should pick him over Chandler
jokes.punchline: Rachel's brutally honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered insult disguised as a compliment. Rachel's honesty makes it cringe in the best way.

jokes.text: That's why I'm marrying you.
jokes.setup: Mike correctly guesses that 'lorkins' means orchids
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's over-the-top declaration of love for his translation skills
jokes.explanation: Sweet Phoebe moment that's endearing rather than laugh-out-loud funny.

jokes.text: Family Honor 2: This A-Time It's A-Personal.
jokes.setup: Joey admits he's borrowing lines from a bad audition
jokes.punchline: The terrible fake Italian accent in the movie title
jokes.explanation: Great fake movie title that perfectly captures Joey's bad acting career. The broken English accent is spot-on.

jokes.text: That must have been one lousy movie. That was me!
jokes.setup: Mike comments on the lousy movie after Joey's speech about family
jokes.punchline: Joey reveals that was him speaking, not the movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Joey's sincere moment. The structure works well with good character integration.

jokes.text: I mean, this guy would strike out and then run to third.
jokes.setup: Chandler talks about being picked last behind a foreign exchange student
jokes.punchline: Specific example of the student's baseball ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail that makes the general joke more vivid. The wrong direction adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Make 'groom' for Chandler.
jokes.setup: Chandler celebrates being chosen as groomsman
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'make room' and 'groom'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that works contextually but isn't particularly clever or surprising.

jokes.text: How bad do you wanna stick your tongue on that?
jokes.setup: Large ice sculpture appears in the hallway
jokes.punchline: Impulse to lick the ice sculpture like a flagpole
jokes.explanation: Relatable childhood impulse but not particularly sophisticated humor.

jokes.text: I thought it would be fun if the third groomsman was my family dog, Chappy.
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler are fighting over groomsman spot
jokes.punchline: Mike suggests his dog as alternative
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist solution to the conflict. Sets up later dog-related comedy.

jokes.text: I think I'm gonna have to go with the dog.
jokes.setup: Rachel has to choose between Ross, Chandler, and now the dog
jokes.punchline: She picks the dog over both men
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that insults both Ross and Chandler while being completely logical.

jokes.text: I want you to be crazy bitch again.
jokes.setup: Phoebe is overwhelmed and asks Monica for help
jokes.punchline: Direct request for Monica to be controlling again
jokes.explanation: Brilliant role reversal where Monica's negative trait becomes desperately needed. Perfect economy and character work.

jokes.text: Since they hired a very hot weather girl.
jokes.setup: Monica asks when Joey started watching the news
jokes.punchline: Joey's shallow reason for news consumption
jokes.explanation: Very Joey response but predictable. Works for character consistency rather than surprise.

jokes.text: I crushed a pill and put it in her drink.
jokes.setup: Mike's mom seems unusually pleased with the street wedding
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of drugging his mother
jokes.explanation: Shocking dark humor delivered casually. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier and more disturbing.

jokes.text: He can sense my fear! My throat is exposed!
jokes.setup: Chandler tries to walk the dog despite being scared
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dramatic reaction to small dog
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Chandler's characteristic over-dramatization. The throat comment adds specificity.

jokes.text: Yeah. You think I'd give up being a minister and pay to ride the subway?
jokes.setup: Joey confirms he's still ordained from Monica and Chandler's wedding
jokes.punchline: His practical reason for keeping ordination is subway fare
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - spiritual calling reduced to financial practicality. Great character-specific humor.

jokes.text: I had to read the Bible pretty carefully, but, yeah, we do.
jokes.setup: Ross questions if ministers really ride subway for free
jokes.punchline: Joey's claim he researched this biblical loophole
jokes.explanation: Funny extension of Joey's misunderstanding. The idea he studied the Bible for subway benefits is perfectly Joey.

jokes.text: I have you scheduled for nudity at 2300 hours.
jokes.setup: Chandler says he wants Monica to wear the headset in bed
jokes.punchline: Monica's military-time scheduling of sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment combining her control issues with military time callback. The clinical scheduling of intimacy is spot-on.

jokes.text: Jeez, Ross. You could've showered. - It's the dog.
jokes.setup: Someone comments on Ross's smell
jokes.punchline: Ross reveals it's actually the dog that smells
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke that works but isn't particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: I'll be my 'something blue.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe worries about being cold in her dress
jokes.punchline: Her blue skin from cold will be her 'something blue'
jokes.explanation: Clever wedding tradition reference with good wordplay. Phoebe's positive spin on freezing is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: The cold has now spread to my special place... so I'm gonna do the short version of this.
jokes.setup: Joey is performing outdoor wedding ceremony in snow
jokes.punchline: Admits the cold has affected his genitals
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the cold weather running gag. Joey's oversharing is in character and creates urgency.

jokes.text: Chappy's heart rate has slowed way down.
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony is happening in freezing weather
jokes.punchline: Casual observation that the dog might be dying
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly during romantic moment. Good contrast and timing.

jokes.text: Could someone get me a coat? I'm frigging freezing.
jokes.setup: Phoebe just got married in beautiful snowy ceremony
jokes.punchline: Immediate return to practical concerns after romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of romantic moment with Phoebe's practical nature. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: I'm about to have the most organized sex anyone's ever had.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions going home at 2300 hours
jokes.punchline: Her characteristic over-organization applied to intimacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment. The phrase 'organized sex' is both funny and completely believable for her character.

jokes.text: Come on, Chappy! Do your business! Make!
jokes.setup: Ross is stuck walking the dog after the wedding
jokes.punchline: His desperate commands to the dog to defecate
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy ending. Ross's frustration is relatable but not particularly clever.
